title: ORS preparation + dehydration danger-signs / when-to-seek-care (clinical)
context: ORS preparation and dehydration danger-signs is the highest-risk content in wash-guides. An incorrect ORS ratio or missed danger-sign can directly harm a person, particularly an infant or young child. This unit is hard-gated on a secured credentialed expert reviewer (partner-014) and the CI HIGH-risk gate (ci-018). No expert = no start. Every numeric value (ORS ratio, volumes, timings) must be independently second-checked and expert-confirmed. Scope is strictly household preparation and when-to-seek-care; no diagnosis, no medication, no IV instruction.
objective: Author, source-cite, independently numeric-verify, expert-confirm, and publish a WashUnit covering ORS (oral rehydration solution) preparation at home and dehydration danger-signs / when-to-seek-care, with every numeric value tied to a specific authoritative source, independently second-checked, and confirmed by a licensed clinician; includes the required 'informational, not a substitute for professional medical care' disclaimer and explicit danger-signs content.
acceptanceCriteria: Every numeric value (ORS ratio, water volume, salt/sugar amounts, timing) is tied to a specific named authoritative source (WHO/UNICEF) with retrieval date and verified license; no value is stated without a source reference

Every numeric value is independently second-checked by a person other than the author (numericClaims[].verifiedBy is distinct from the author) and confirmed by the secured licensed clinician (expertConfirmed:true on every numericClaim)

A recorded expertSignOff from the secured licensed clinician (partner-014) is attached to this content version before merge; the sign-off is version-scoped (attaches to a specific content version and citation/numeric set)
Unit includes explicit danger-signs / when-to-seek-care content (signs of severe dehydration, situations requiring immediate medical attention) sourced and clinician-confirmed
Unit carries a persistent 'This is general information, not a substitute for professional medical care; seek qualified help for serious symptoms or uncertainty' disclaimer in the rendered output
Scope is enforced: no diagnosis, no IV instruction, no medication dosing beyond ORS; borderline cases (e.g., ORS for infants) handled per the expert-confirmed scope line and linked to care
All source text is paraphrased — no verbatim copied WHO/UNICEF text; no logos/emblems; CI copy/similarity check passes
CI HIGH-risk gate (ci-018) passes: expertSignOff present, all numericClaims have verifiedBy and expertConfirmed:true, disclaimer and danger-signs fields present
Reviewed and approved by a WASH/public-health domain reviewer (not the author or the expert); PR-tied review-log entry written
output: A committed, clinician-signed-off, independently numeric-verified, source-cited WashUnit for ORS preparation and dehydration danger-signs, with recorded expertSignOff, all numericClaims verified and expert-confirmed, and the required disclaimer and danger-signs content.
